author: eharris128
description: Enables access to LLMs via remote providers (OpenAI, Claude, Gemini) and local LLMs via GPT4ALL.

manifest.author: eharris128, r-mahoney, & jsmorabito
manifest.description: Chat with LLMs (OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, Mistral, Ollama, LM Studio) directly in your notes. Includes Skills, Projects, Assistants, Memory, vault search, voice transcription, and an AI Agent that can read and write your vault.
manifest.name: Large Language Models

name: Large Language Models

releases.author: jsmorabito
releases.description: - fix: update standing horse pixel art sprite

releases.author: jsmorabito
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

- **SDK install node path** — adds Homebrew (Apple Silicon `/opt/homebrew/bin`), MacPorts, and nodenv to the node/npm binary search paths so the Claude Code runtime SDK installer works on more macOS setups

releases.author: jsmorabito
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

- **SDK install ENOENT fix** — when npm is at a non-standard path not found by the installer's path resolver, the node binary lookup now falls back to an independent search across common install locations (nvm, volta, fnm, asdf, system paths) instead of failing with `spawn node ENOENT`

releases.author: jsmorabito
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

- **Ask AI popup multi-vault fix** — the status bar Ask AI popup and recent chats popover now correctly attach to the current vault's window document. Previously, if another vault had focus when the plugin loaded, the popup element was created in the wrong window and clicking the button had no effect.

releases.author: jsmorabito
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### Bug Fixes
- **Claude Code SDK install in settings** — the one-time runtime SDK download (~69 MB) is now surfaced as a "Download SDK" button under Settings → Anthropic → Claude Code, instead of appearing as an error when trying to send a message
- **FAB and Ask AI popup regression** — fixed FAB and status bar Ask AI popup not appearing after plugin load
- **ESLint errors resolved** — fixed unnecessary type assertions, unused variables, bare console calls, and == vs === comparisons

### Improvements
- **Manifest linter** — `npm run build` now fails fast if `manifest.json` is missing required fields (e.g. version), preventing broken releases
- **Obsidian plugin review** — addressed automated review warnings: replaced deprecated APIs (`activeLeaf`, `setWarning`, `Vault.trash`), switched `fetch` → `requestUrl`, added `void` to unawaited promises, updated manifest metadata

releases.author: jsmorabito
releases.description:
## What's new

- **Robust settings merge** — plugin upgrades now automatically fill in new default values for any settings object without requiring manual wiring. Prevents settings from silently resetting after a reload when new fields are added.

releases.author: jsmorabito
releases.description:
## What's new

- **Custom loading animation** — a hidden setting in Interface that replaces the default thinking dots with a custom pixel animation; also swaps the Obsidian Agent brand icon for a matching static variant. Both inherit your theme's accent color.

releases.author: jsmorabito
releases.description:
## Bug fixes

- **Skills settings panel no longer blank** — the Skills tab in settings was rendering nothing when the skill registry hadn't finished loading (a timing race with Obsidian's `onLayoutReady`) or when no root vault folder was configured. The panel now shows a descriptive message in both empty-registry cases and automatically re-renders once the registry finishes loading. When skills are present, a section heading and description are shown above the toggle rows. This also resolves the slash-menu (`/`) not appearing in the chat input for the same reason — the registry was empty when the menu was first invoked.

releases.author: jsmorabito
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### Obsidian compliance pass ([#293](https://github.com/eharris128/Obsidian-LLM-Plugin/pull/293))
- **Dev-gated logger** — new `src/utils/logger.ts`; `debug/log/info` stripped from production builds; `warn/error` always emit with `[LLM]` prefix. All 75 `console.*` calls across 20 files migrated.
- **`.llm-hidden` utility** — replaced ~55 inline `style.display` reads/writes with class toggles across FAB, StatusBar, ChatContainer, RecentChats, and LLMSettingsModal.
- **Lint** — added `npm run lint` / `lint:fix`; re-enabled `prefer-const`, `eqeqeq`, `no-console` as warnings.
- **`normalizePath()`** on vault folder getters; `instanceof TFile` replacing `as any` casts.
- Smoke tested by @johnny1093.

releases.author: jsmorabito
releases.description:
## What's new

### Claude Code permission prompts in chat
Tool permission requests from Claude Code now appear as interactive Allow/Deny cards directly in the chat window — no more being told to edit `~/.claude/settings.json` or use the terminal. Auto-denied tools surface as an inline notice so you always know when an action was blocked.

releases.author: jsmorabito
releases.description:
Fixes the 0.24.12 release which shipped with an incorrect main.js build artifact. All features from 0.24.12 are included:

- `/attach` slash command — native file/folder picker with context chips
- `read_local_file`, `list_local_folder`, `run_shell_command` tools for the Obsidian Agent
- API key test buttons and Claude Code OAuth token validation in Settings → Anthropic
- Auto-sync Claude Code OAuth token from macOS Keychain on load
- Thinking animation shows active tool name during agent tool calls
- Token usage displayed below Obsidian Agent responses
- Stop button uses subtle styling instead of red
- Widget restores last open chat on plugin reload
- Slash menu positions below prompt when near top of screen

releases.author: jsmorabito
releases.description:
## What's new

### Local filesystem tools
- New tools available to the Obsidian Agent: `read_local_file`, `list_local_folder`, and `run_shell_command`
- Shell commands are **off by default** and require explicit opt-in via a warning dialog in Settings → Tools

### `/attach` slash command
- Type `/` in the chat input to open a native OS file/folder picker
- Selected paths appear as chips above the input and are injected as context at send time
- Hidden files are always visible in the picker (no need for Cmd+Shift+.)
- Attached paths are saved to the chat file and restored when you reopen the conversation

### API key validation & Keychain sync (macOS)
- New "Test" buttons in Settings → Anthropic for both the Claude API key and OAuth token — shows ✓/✗ with the exact error from the API
- Plugin auto-reads the Claude Code OAuth token from the macOS Keychain on load if the field is empty
- "Sync from Keychain" button for manual refresh after re-authenticating with the Claude Code CLI

### Agent UX improvements
- Thinking animation now shows **"Running: \<tool name\>"** during tool execution
- Token usage displayed below every Obsidian Agent response
- Attached vault notes and local folders are labeled as pre-loaded context so the agent doesn't re-read them with tools

### Other fixes
- Slash menu now positions below the prompt when the chat window is near the top of the screen
- Stop generation button is now subtle white/grey instead of red
- Widget restores the last open chat after plugin reload

releases.author: jsmorabito
releases.description:
## Fix: Claude Code model now works correctly

The Claude Code model was showing a persistent 'one-time download' notice and failing silently. Root cause: `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` no longer ships `cli.js` — the SDK now distributes platform-specific native binaries via optional packages (`claude-agent-sdk-darwin-arm64`, etc.).

- Fixed `isSDKInstalled` to check for the native binary instead of the missing `cli.js`
- Installer now explicitly installs the platform-specific binary package alongside the SDK
- Bumped SDK to `0.3.170`
- Removed the `node` path-resolution workaround (native binary spawns directly)

releases.author: jsmorabito
releases.description:
## Bug fixes

- **No more silent failures when API calls fail** — missing key, invalid key, insufficient credits, and other API errors now all surface as actionable notices instead of being silently swallowed
- **Insufficient credits** now shows: *"Your API credit balance is too low. Add credits at console.anthropic.com/settings/billing."*
- **Agent mode API key check** — sending a message in agent mode with no API key configured now immediately shows a clear error notice rather than throwing internally
- **Obsidian Agent toggle sync** — enabling the agent from the Obsidian Agent settings tab now correctly updates the StatusBar chat container (previously only the FAB was updated)
- **Claude models in agent settings** — entering an Anthropic API key now immediately makes Claude models available in the Agent model dropdown without needing to navigate away and back; clearing a key is also now properly saved

releases.author: jsmorabito
releases.description:
## ONNX local embeddings — now working in-process

Vault search with the ONNX provider (no server required) is now fully functional. The model runs directly inside Obsidian using native CPU bindings.

### How it works
- **One-time download (~110 MB)** — `Xenova/all-mpnet-base-v2` weights are fetched from HuggingFace and cached to disk on first use
- **Runs in-process** — uses `onnxruntime-node` native bindings already bundled with Obsidian, no subprocess or external server needed
- **768-dimensional embeddings** — competitive with OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` on semantic similarity benchmarks
- **Fully offline after first download**

### Also in this release
- Fix model status label (was showing `nomic-embed-text-v1.5`, now correctly shows `all-mpnet-base-v2`)
- Add `onnx-models/` to `.gitignore` to prevent accidental commit of downloaded model weights

releases.author: jsmorabito
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

- **Embedding provider switch now re-indexes automatically** — switching from Ollama to ONNX (or any provider change) clears the old index and rebuilds it immediately, preventing stale vectors from corrupting search results
- **Short notes are now indexed** — files with fewer than 50 characters of content (e.g. a simple "About me" note) were silently skipped; they are now always included
- **Vault search sources panel shows grep results** — the collapsible "N sources" panel after a response now tracks notes found via `grep_vault` in addition to `search_vault_semantic`
- **Ollama/LM Studio model refresh fixed** — the Refresh button in provider settings now uses the same HTTP stack as chat requests, resolving failures that required an Obsidian restart to work around

releases.author: jsmorabito
releases.description: Fix: ONNX model download now works in Obsidian. Sets transformers.js cache dir to the plugin's OS path, forcing Node.js https (not browser fetch) — bypasses Obsidian's Content Security Policy that was blocking huggingface.co.
releases.name: v0.24.7 — Fix ONNX model download (CSP bypass)

releases.author: jsmorabito
releases.description: Fix: restore missing `version` field in manifest.json that was dropped during a merge. This was preventing Obsidian from recognizing the plugin update.
releases.name: v0.24.6 — Fix missing version in manifest.json

releases.author: jsmorabito
releases.description:
## What's New

### Embedding provider choice

Vault Search now lets you pick your embedding backend. **ONNX remains the default** (no server, runs in-process), but you can switch to any of the original external providers in **Settings → Embeddings**.

| Provider | Notes |
|----------|-------|
| **ONNX** (default) | Xenova/nomic-embed-text-v1.5, q8 quantized, ~90 MB download, fully offline after first use |
| **Ollama** | Uses your configured Ollama host; model must be pulled (e.g. `ollama pull nomic-embed-text`) |
| **OpenAI** | Uses your OpenAI API key; default model `text-embedding-3-small` |
| **Gemini** | Uses your Gemini API key; default model `text-embedding-004` |
| **LM Studio** | Uses your LM Studio host; load an embedding model in LM Studio first |

### Settings UI

- New **Embedding provider** dropdown in the Embeddings tab
- ONNX selection shows model download status and a Download/Load button
- External provider selections show an editable model name field and a prerequisite hint (which API key or server is used)

### Migration

No re-indexing required when switching between 768-dim models (ONNX nomic-embed-text-v1.5, Ollama nomic-embed-text). Re-index if you switch to a different output dimension (e.g. OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` = 1536-dim).
releases.name: v0.24.5 — Multi-provider embeddings (ONNX + Ollama/OpenAI/Gemini/LM Studio)

releases.author: jsmorabito
releases.description:
## What's New

### ONNX Local Embeddings — Vault Search No Longer Requires Ollama

Vault Search (RAG) now runs its embedding model **entirely in-process** using [@huggingface/transformers](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers.js). No external server needed.

**Model:** Xenova/nomic-embed-text-v1.5 (q8 quantized, ~90MB)
- 768-dim output — fully compatible with existing \`rag-index.json\` vectors (no re-index required)
- Downloads once on first enable, then runs fully offline
- Instant load on subsequent sessions once cached

### Changes

- **EmbeddingService** rewritten as an ONNX singleton; removed OpenAI/Gemini/Ollama/LM Studio provider routing
- **Settings UI** — replaced provider/model dropdowns with a model status indicator and a Download/Load button with live progress reporting
- **RAGSettings type** — removed \`embeddingProvider\`/\`embeddingModel\`, added \`modelCached\` flag
- Plugin auto-warms the model on load when RAG is enabled and the model is cached

### Upgrading

Existing \`rag-index.json\` files created with \`nomic-embed-text\` via Ollama are **dimension-compatible** — no re-indexing required after upgrading.

If you previously used a different embedding model (non-768-dim), use the **Re-index Vault** button in settings after upgrading.
releases.name: v0.24.4 — ONNX Local Embeddings (No Ollama Required)

releases.author: jsmorabito
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### Tool Category Disambiguation
Small models (especially local 9B-parameter models) were conflating `grep_vault`/`search_vault_semantic` with `web_search` — calling vault search tools to follow up on web search results. The system prompt now explicitly states that those tools are local-vault-only and directs the model to use `web_search` for anything internet-facing.

### Learning Loop
The agent can now learn persistent user preferences mid-conversation. When a user says something like "remember that", "always", "never", "from now on", or "I prefer", the agent will update the configured agent guidance file with the new preference and confirm the update. If no guidance file is configured, the agent will prompt the user to set one in plugin settings. One-off or task-specific instructions are not written to the guidance file — only persistent preferences.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### Fix: Context Window Budget Calculation

The "Context token budget (%)" setting was broken by design — the budget was calculated as `maxTokens × contextPercent / 100`, where `maxTokens` was the **response length** setting (defaulting to 300), not the model's actual context window. This meant only ~210 tokens were ever budgeted for injected context, regardless of whether the model supports 200,000.

**Changes:**
- Added `contextWindow` to the `Model` type and populated accurate values for all known models (Claude: 200k, GPT-4.1: 1M, Gemini: 1M, Mistral: 131k, GPT4All/Ollama: 8k fallback)
- Context budget is now calculated from the model's true context window size
- Ollama models: clicking **Refresh** in settings now queries `/api/show` for each model's `num_ctx` and persists it — so the budget reflects that model's actual loaded context size
- Added `ContextBuilder.estimateTokens()` static helper (chars ÷ 4 heuristic) for reuse

---

### Feature: Smart Response Length

The "Tokens" setting was a flat cap (default 300) that truncated most meaningful cloud model responses. It now works intelligently based on model type.

**Cloud models (OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, Mistral):**
- By default, **no `max_tokens` cap is sent** — the model stops naturally when done
- The model's `maxOutputTokens` acts as a safety ceiling if a user override is set
- Claude's internal fallback raised from 4,096 → 64,000 (the API requires the field)

**Local models (Ollama, GPT4All):**
- Response cap is computed dynamically as `contextWindow − contextTokenBudget`, floored at 1,024
- Prevents the model from running past its window while maximizing response space

**User override:**
- Set "Max response tokens" to **0** (default/recommended) for smart automatic behavior
- Set a positive number to enforce a hard cap — for local models, the lower of your override and the dynamic cap wins; for cloud models, it's clamped to the model's maximum

**Setting renamed:** "Tokens" → "Max response tokens" with an updated description.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/eharris128/Obsidian-LLM-Plugin/compare/0.24.1...0.24.2

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed
* Fix plugin load crash (import.meta.url) + add local E2E suite by @eharris128 in https://github.com/eharris128/Obsidian-LLM-Plugin/pull/291


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/eharris128/Obsidian-LLM-Plugin/compare/0.23.3...0.24.1

releases.author: jsmorabito
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### Bug Fix: Plugin load crash on fresh install

`@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` calls `createRequire(import.meta.url)` at module scope. esbuild's CJS output stubs `import.meta` to `{}`, causing `createRequire` to throw on plugin load:

```
TypeError [ERR_INVALID_ARG_VALUE]: The argument 'filename' must be a file URL object,
file URL string, or absolute path string. Received undefined
```

This caused **the whole plugin to fail to load** on a fresh install (reproduced on Obsidian 1.12.7). Fixed with a `define` + banner shim in `esbuild.config.mjs` that derives `import.meta.url` from `__filename`.

### New: Local E2E test suite

`npm run test:e2e` builds the plugin, stages it into a sandbox vault, and runs 13 Mocha specs against a real downloaded Obsidian instance via [wdio-obsidian-service](https://github.com/jesse-r-s-hines/wdio-obsidian-service). First run downloads Obsidian (~150 MB, cached); after that the suite runs in ~6 seconds. No real vault, no API keys.

Coverage: plugin load, core commands, widget chat UI, slash menu, chat modal, chats panel, chat details panel, header settings toggle.

releases.author: eharris128
releases.description:
## What's New

### Features
- **Updated ninja cat icon** — Fresh new ninja cat design

### Model & Provider Updates
- **Claude 4.5/4.6 models** — Added Claude Sonnet 4.6, Opus 4.6, and Haiku 4.5
- **Updated provider docs** — Refreshed provider support info for Ollama, Mistral, and new models

### Bug Fixes
- **Ollama model registration** — Models now register globally after refresh, with request timeout support
- **Settings persistence** — Deep-merge view settings so contextSettings persists across sessions
- **Header bar redesign** — Implemented header bar design changes from #167

### Maintenance
- **Removed deprecated features** — Removed OpenAI Assistants feature
- **Removed deprecated models** — Removed Gemini 1.5 Flash, Gemini 2.0 Flash exp/thinking, and Claude 3.5 Sonnet June
- **Pinned agent SDK** — Pinned @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk to 0.2.73
- **Security** — npm audit fix
- **Cleanup** — Removed unused DRY refactoring snapshot files

releases.author: eharris128
releases.description:
## What's New

### New Model Support
- **Claude 4.5/4.6 models** — Added Claude Sonnet 4.6, Opus 4.6, and Haiku 4.5
- **New OpenAI models** — Added GPT-4o-mini, GPT-4.1, o3, o4-mini, and gpt-image-1
- **Ollama provider** — Local LLM support via Ollama
- **Mistral AI provider** — New remote provider integration

### Image Generation
- **gpt-image-1 support** — Replaced DALL-E 2/3 with OpenAI's latest image generation model
- **Fixed b64_json handling** — gpt-image-1 returns base64 data instead of URLs; images now render correctly
- **Improved quality settings** — Simplified image quality validation with sensible defaults

### Bug Fixes
- **Gemini 429 rate limits** — Better error messages and retry with exponential backoff for rate-limited requests
- **Mistral API compatibility** — Strip x-stainless-* headers that caused CORS failures
- **Gemini API key validation** — Use gemini-2.0-flash for validation checks
- **max_tokens optional** — OpenAI models now use API defaults when max_tokens is unset, preventing errors with models that don't accept it

### Other
- User-selectable avatar SVG for empty new chat
- Ribbon icon toggle setting

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/eharris128/Obsidian-LLM-Plugin/compare/0.21.0...0.22.0

releases.author: eharris128
releases.description:
## What's New

### Auto-install Claude Agent SDK at runtime
In release builds, the Claude Agent SDK's `cli.js` and runtime files (~69 MB) aren't shipped with the plugin. This release adds automatic one-time installation via `npm install` on first use of Claude Code.

- **Zero overhead after first install** — skips immediately if `cli.js` already exists
- **Persistent notice** during download so the user knows what's happening
- **Concurrency guard** prevents duplicate installs if triggered from multiple views
- **2-minute timeout** with clear error messaging if install fails
- **Cross-platform npm resolution** (nvm, volta, fnm, asdf, system paths, Windows support)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/eharris128/Obsidian-LLM-Plugin/compare/0.20.0...0.21.0

releases.author: eharris128
releases.description:
## ✨ Features

- **Auto-focus chat input**: The chat input field now automatically receives focus when you open the modal, widget, or FAB. You can start typing immediately without needing to click the input first! (#258)

## 🐛 Bug Fixes

- **Fix duplicate message display**: Fixed an issue where user messages would briefly appear duplicated when sent, with one instance incorrectly positioned on the right side like an assistant message (#258)

## 🔧 Technical Improvements

- Set `tabIndex=0` on chat input for proper keyboard navigation
- Optimized message rendering to prevent subscription and manual append conflicts

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/eharris128/Obsidian-LLM-Plugin/compare/0.19.20...0.20.0

releases.author: r-mahoney
releases.description: Should now persist any user changes made to settings instead of reapplying default settings on every reload of plugin. Also should fix a bug seen where settings were not populating correctly causing settings view to crash trying to set field values from user settings.

releases.author: eharris128
releases.description:
- Add [no history view](https://github.com/r-mahoney/Obsidian-LLM-Plugin/pull/145)
- Update design for the [settings page](https://github.com/r-mahoney/Obsidian-LLM-Plugin/pull/143)
- Add handling for an invalid [chatgpt key](https://github.com/r-mahoney/Obsidian-LLM-Plugin/pull/142)
- Add support for regenerating messages on the [history view](https://github.com/r-mahoney/Obsidian-LLM-Plugin/pull/141)

releases.author: r-mahoney
releases.description: Fixes bugs where selected files were not being added to the array of assistant files for assistant creation

releases.author: r-mahoney
releases.description: Bug fixes where initial install would cause plugin to crash do to being unable to add an api key before api call on plugin load

releases.author: r-mahoney
releases.description:
Major update to assistants tab:
-Added drop down to allow users to create/update/delete assistants and vector stores
-current functionality is for create/delete assistants and delete vector stores
-updated file search functionality to make it easier for users to add files to assistants

releases.author: r-mahoney
releases.description: Revert model dropdown changes that are breaking model selections.

releases.author: r-mahoney
releases.description: Implements potential bug fix caused by non-specific file checking when adding files to assistants.

releases.author: r-mahoney
releases.description: Release comes with File Search functionality through the assistants tooling offered by OpenAI. The new button in the header of each view will allow you to create an assistant and upload files from your vault in order to interact with an LLM on the content in those files. Adding files is done by typing out the name of the note followed by ".md" for the file extension. For example, a note named "Daily Planning" should be input into the text are box as 'Daily Planning.md". Then the assistant will show up in the list of models to choose from in the view settings and you can chat with it about your notes like you would chat with any other LLM.

releases.author: r-mahoney
releases.description:
Still bug fixing. 
Fix for this bug is either deleting data.json for settings to be refreshed, or fresh install on local comp

releases.author: r-mahoney
releases.description:
Updates FAB header to include a closing button to match style guide.
Updates response styling so that anything besides text gets rendered as markdown and styled correctly

releases.author: r-mahoney
releases.description:
Add some settings to allow users to disable/enable the FAB if they don't want that functionality. Added donate button to settings page. Added SVG and credits to settings page. 

Added some safeguards to prevent users form spamming prompts to GPT4All backend which would break UI.

Fixed bugs where chat index wasn't updating causing some chats to not be saved in certain views.

releases.author: r-mahoney
releases.description: CSS changes

releases.author: r-mahoney
releases.description: Updates FAB styling to Johns specs. Regenerates settings container so that settings should never be out of sync with what is displayed

releases.author: r-mahoney
releases.description: still attempting to fix input visual bug

releases.description: Fixing a visual bug with the FAB chat prompt text area where the button wouldn't be centered inside the text area

releases.description: Implements FAB functionality

releases.author: r-mahoney
releases.description: Release should fix bug where history does not show up in chat modal

releases.author: r-mahoney
releases.description: fixed bug where only default model was able to be used

releases.author: r-mahoney
releases.description:
Fixed bugs:
-chat history not updating after asking a question. Modal had to be closed and reopened for history to show up
-model not saving to history if user changes model after initial chat
-Modal and Widget use default settings when first opened in order to prevent inconsistent settings behavior

Implemented:
-Widget view

Widget View:
-implemented custom css logic to be able to easily change modal and widget css
-extracted logic out of modal in order to make pieces reusable for widget view and eventual fab
-implemented individual settings for widget and modal so changing one doesnt affect the other

releases.author: r-mahoney
releases.description:
Releases DEV-5. Releases DEV-52. Releases DEV-53. Releases DEV-58. Releases DEV-61.  Releases DEV-62. Releases DEV-63.

Updated button stylings to better reflect the overall Obsidian styles. Back arrows removed from button states. Added loading animation as a placeholder for GPT4All models to add a better user experience until streaming responses can be implemented for local LLMs. Added openAI models and a setting to add an openAI API key. OpenAI responses get streamed to response div for good UX/UI.

releases.author: r-mahoney
releases.description: Lots of new changes to be tested. Header icons and active state should update depending on which button you click/double click. Chat history items should now display prompt and used model in header when selecting item. Changing model to use changes header.

releases.author: r-mahoney
releases.description: Updated history container functionality. Allows users to edit titles of prompts and saves to chat history.

releases.author: r-mahoney
releases.description: Updated UI. No longer have same checks in place for note open and models downloaded/gpt4all open, still needs implementation. Pushing release for John to make comments.

releases.description: Potentially fixing a bug where model directory was wrong for Macs.

releases.author: r-mahoney
releases.description: Cleaned up some of the UI in the chat modal. Removed the conversational chat ribbon icon that was only for testing. Added a command to open the chat modal. Added history functionality that lets you pull up old prompts

releases.author: r-mahoney
releases.description: Added the ability to converse with the chosen language model. Still need to add functionality to add chats to notes after generation and add a "go back button" in case the user wants to start a new conversation. Pushing beta BRAT release for john to play with functionality
